content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.3 `PredicateCatalog` .. 3.4 `Note`
Document lines: 168-310

### 3.3 `PredicateCatalog`

Это не memory item, а конфиг для reconciliation.

Почему нужен:
- у разных предикатов разная cardinality
- у разных предикатов разная temporal semantics
- одни факты заменяются, другие сосуществуют
- часть facts идет в profile projection

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `predicate: text primary key`
- `description: text`
- `subject_type: text`
- `object_kind: entity | string | number | boolean | json`
- `cardinality: single | multi`
- `temporal_policy: atemporal | time_scoped | status_like`
- `conflict_policy: replace | coexist | range_split`
- `profile_sync: boolean`
- `task_sync: boolean`
- `default_importance: smallint`
- `active: boolean`

Примеры:
- `preferred_name`
- `speaks_language`
- `prefers_response_language`
- `prefers_code_comment_language`
- `works_with`
- `has_goal`
- `works_on_project`
- `uses_database`
- `has_constraint`

---

### 3.4 `Note`

Промежуточный semantic memory fragment.

Это:
- не source
- не final claim
- не chain-of-thought

`Note` нужен для вещей, которые:
- уже важны
- полезны для retrieval
- но еще не должны быть зацементированы как hard fact

Типичные кейсы:
- design direction
- decision rationale
- evolving plans
- hypotheses
- lessons / tactics / patterns
- doc digests
- contextual framing

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `note_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id`
- `note_type: decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context`
- `title: text`
- `summary: text`
- `scope_text: text | null`
- `scope_json: jsonb`
- `status: active | superseded | retracted | resolved | stale | candidate`
- `retrieval_state: active | warm | cold | archived | deleted`
- `maturity_state: fresh | stabilizing | mature | consolidated`
- `maturity_score: numeric(4,3)`
- `anchor_entity_id: uuid | null`
- `keywords_json: jsonb`
- `tags_json: jsonb`
- `candidate_claims_json: jsonb`
- `metadata_json: jsonb`
- `confidence: numeric(4,3)`
- `importance: smallint`
- `valid_from: timestamptz | null`
- `valid_to: timestamptz | null`
- `supersedes_note_id: uuid | null`
- `created_from_run_id: uuid | null`
- `created_at: timestamptz`
- `updated_at: timestamptz`
- `embedding: vector | null`
- `use_count: integer`
- `last_used_at: timestamptz | null`
- `last_validated_at: timestamptz | null`
- `evidence_count: integer`
- `archived_at: timestamptz | null`

Дополнительные таблицы:

#### `note_entities`
- `note_id`
- `entity_id`
- `entity_role: primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject`

#### `note_sources`
- `note_id`
- `source_id`
- `support_type: direct | summarized | imported`
- `evidence_span`
- `evidence_quote`
- `support_confidence`

#### `note_links`
- `from_note_id`
- `to_note_id`
- `link_type: supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from`
- `link_weight`

Инварианты `Note`:
- одна note = один semantic fragment
- note должна быть понятна без исходного чата
- note не дублирует source без added value
- `candidate_claims_json` — только hints
- note может жить долго без превращения в claim, если полезен для retrieval

Пример:

```json
{
  "note_type": "decision",
  "title": "Use Postgres as memory source of truth",
  "summary": "The current direction is to keep Postgres with pgvector as the main memory store because it keeps structured memory, retrieval metadata, and transactions together. Neo4j is deferred until traversal-heavy use cases become core.",
  "scope_text": "Agent memory layer for the current product iteration",
  "scope_json": {
    "scope_kind": "project",
    "project_id": "agent_memory",
    "modality": "explicit"
  },
  "status": "active",
  "retrieval_state": "active",
  "maturity_state": "stabilizing",
  "maturity_score": 0.62,
  "confidence": 0.88,
  "importance": 8
}
```

---

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=`PredicateCatalog` is a reconciliation config, not a memory item summary=In the v3 data model, `PredicateCatalog` is defined as configuration for reconciliation logic rather than a stored memory item; it encodes per-predicate rules such as cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict handling, and projection sync flags. scope=Applies to section 3.3 `PredicateCatalog` in 02. Data Model (agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md) entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1> keywords=PredicateCatalog,reconciliation,cardinality,temporal_policy,conflict_policy tags=data_model,reconciliation_config,v3 evidence=Это не memory item, а конфиг для reconciliation.
[1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=`Note` entity role, boundaries, and invariants in v3 summary=`Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment used for important retrieval-useful context that should not yet be hardened into final claims; it excludes source records and chain-of-thought, and follows invariants like one semantic fragment per note and self-contained meaning. scope=Applies to section 3.4 `Note` in 02. Data Model (agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md) entities=entity:<2>,entity:<1> keywords=Note,intermediate memory,retrieval,invariants,candidate_claims_json tags=data_model,note_semantics,v3 evidence=Промежуточный semantic memory fragment.

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Cross-cutting policy: scope, retrieval lifecycle, and usage telemetry summary=In v3, scope and retrieval lifecycle are explicit cross-cutting concerns: use dual scope fields (`scope_text` for explainability and `scope_json` for retrieval/reconciliation), keep `truth status` separate from `retrieval_state`, apply retrieval states active/warm/cold/archived/deleted, and track usage fields (`use_count`, `last_used_at`, `last_validated_at`) to support maturity, retention, and re...[truncated 5 chars] scope=Applies to cross-cutting fields defined in '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' keywords=scope_text,scope_json,retrieval_state,truth status,usage fields tags=cross_cutting,retrieval_lifecycle,schema_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 memory model layers and design goals summary=The v3 data model is designed to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability by splitting memory into layers: Source (evidence), Entity/Claim (semantic graph), Note (intermediate), Profile/Task/Episode (operational projections), and MemoryRun (audit). scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=v3 data model,memory layers,typed memory,lineage,temporal truth tags=data_model,architecture,memory_v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Source` component role and invariants in v3 data model summary=In section 3.1, `Source` is defined as the raw knowledge/evidence layer that stores chat/tool/imported/external records and supports re-check/explainability; it should be immutable in practice, keep `observed_at` distinct from ingestion time, use `content_hash` for dedupe, and enforce explicit deletion policy. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Source,evidence layer,observed_at,content_hash,delete policy tags=data_model,memory_v3,source_component useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Entity` as canonical memory node with alias/merge semantics summary=In section 3.2, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions of the same object and anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes; it uses aliases and merge status while preserving history, keeping aliases pointing to the current canonical entity, and allowing erroneous merges to be reversed via audit trail. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Entity,canonical node,entity_aliases,merge,audit trail tags=data_model,memory_v3,entity_component useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
